cases.input: I am treating a 55-year-old male with a 6 cm rectal cancer (Stage III, T3N1M0) 3 cm from the anal verge. He has severe obstructive sleep apnea requiring CPAP. He is morbidly obese (BMI 45). He wants to preserve sphincter function. Should I recommend neoadjuvant chemoradiation followed by sphincter-sparing surgery, or should I recommend APR? How does obesity affect neoadjuvant radiation delivery? Could brachytherapy boost improve complete response rates for watch-and-wait? What are the anesthetic considerations?
cases.description: Complex decision for rectal cancer in obese patient wanting sphincter preservation

cases.input: I have a 70-year-old female with a 2 cm endometrial cancer (Grade 1, Stage IA) who has severe dementia (MMSE 12). Her family wants definitive treatment. She has a well-functioning cardiac pacemaker. Given her cognitive impairment and pacemaker, should I recommend definitive brachytherapy or observation? How do I manage the pacemaker during radiation therapy? What are the ethical considerations for treating a cognitively impaired patient? Should I use hypofractionation to minimize treatment visits?
cases.description: Treatment decision for cognitively impaired patient with pacemaker

cases.input: I am evaluating a 40-year-old male with a 3 cm penile cancer (squamous cell, Stage T2N0M0) who had a partial penectomy. The surgical margin is 5 mm. He wants organ preservation and is willing to accept a higher recurrence risk. Should I recommend adjuvant radiation to the penile stump? What is the role of brachytherapy for adjuvant treatment? What fractionation would minimize long-term complications while providing adequate coverage? What is the evidence for adjuvant radiation after partial penectomy?
cases.description: Adjuvant therapy decision for penile cancer after partial penectomy

cases.input: I have a 55-year-old female with a 4 cm vaginal cancer (squamous cell, Stage III) who has a spinal cord stimulator for chronic pain. The stimulator battery is implanted in the buttock with leads in the spinal canal. How does the spinal cord stimulator affect radiation therapy planning? Should I recommend brachytherapy to minimize radiation to the device? What are the radiation tolerance limits for the spinal cord stimulator? Should I recommend device removal before treatment?
cases.description: Treatment decision complicated by implanted spinal cord stimulator

cases.input: I am treating a 60-year-old male with prostate cancer (Gleason 4+3, Stage T2c, PSA 12) who has a history of bilateral deep vein thrombosis. He is on lifelong anticoagulation with rivaroxaban. He wants LDR brachytherapy. How should I manage his anticoagulation during the implant procedure? Should I use bridging therapy? What INR or anti-Xa level is safe for perineal needle insertion? Should I recommend EBRT instead to avoid bleeding risk?
cases.description: Treatment decision for prostate cancer with anticoagulation requirement

cases.input: I have a 45-year-old female with a 2 cm breast cancer (Stage I, triple negative) who had a lumpectomy with close margins (1 mm). She refuses re-excision and wants breast-conserving therapy. She is BRCA1 positive with a strong family history. Given the triple negative histology and BRCA status, should I recommend brachytherapy boost? What are the local recurrence rates for triple negative breast cancer with brachytherapy? Should I recommend mastectomy instead?
cases.description: Decision about brachytherapy for triple negative breast cancer with close margin

cases.input: I am evaluating a 75-year-old male with a 3 cm prostate cancer (Gleason 3+4, Stage T2b, PSA 9.5) who has a life expectancy of 4-6 years due to metastatic lung cancer. He wants quality-of-life focused treatment. Should I recommend active surveillance, LDR brachytherapy, or observation? Given his limited life expectancy from lung cancer, what is the appropriate level of prostate cancer treatment? What are the competing risks? How should I counsel him about treatment goals?
cases.description: Treatment decision for prostate cancer with competing metastatic disease

cases.input: I have a 50-year-old female with a 5 cm locally advanced cervical cancer (Stage IIB) who has been treated with concurrent cisplatin-based chemoradiation. At the time of brachytherapy, MRI shows a partial response with a 2 cm residual tumor. The tandem and ovoid applicator does not adequately cover the residual tumor. Should I consider interstitial brachytherapy to improve coverage? What are the benefits and risks of interstitial approach? Should I modify the concurrent chemotherapy during brachytherapy?
cases.description: Decision about interstitial brachytherapy for inadequate applicator coverage

cases.input: I am treating a 65-year-old male with a 4 cm rectal cancer (Stage II, T3N0M0) who has a hip replacement on the left side. He wants to avoid a permanent colostomy. Should I recommend neoadjuvant chemoradiation followed by low anterior resection, or should I consider brachytherapy boost for organ preservation? How does the hip replacement affect radiation treatment planning? What are the dosimetric implications of hip scatter?
cases.description: Decision about rectal cancer treatment with hip replacement complication

cases.input: I have a 40-year-old female with a 3 cm invasive ductal carcinoma of the breast (Stage I, ER+/PR+, HER2-) who had a lumpectomy with clear margins. She is very physically active and a competitive marathon runner. She is concerned about radiation affecting her training and competition schedule. Should I recommend accelerated partial breast irradiation (APBI) with brachytherapy to minimize treatment time? What are the comparative outcomes of APBI vs whole breast radiation for her cancer type? How quickly can she resume intense physical activity after brachytherapy?
cases.description: Patient-centered decision about treatment approach for active patient

cases.input: I am evaluating a 55-year-old male with a 5 cm rectal cancer (Stage IIA, T3N0M0) who has a colostomy from prior diverticular disease perforation. He wants to avoid another abdominal surgery. Should I recommend neoadjuvant chemoradiation followed by resection, or should I consider definitive chemoradiation with brachytherapy boost? Given his prior surgery, what are the adhesions and altered anatomy concerns? How does the colostomy affect radiation treatment planning?
cases.description: Decision about rectal cancer treatment with prior colostomy

cases.input: I have a 60-year-old female with a 2 cm endometrial cancer (Grade 2, Stage IA) who has a history of breast cancer treated with bilateral mastectomy and adjuvant tamoxifen for 5 years. She has been on tamoxifen for 3 years. Given her tamoxifen use, should I consider this as a risk factor? What are the implications of tamoxifen for endometrial cancer treatment? Should I recommend definitive brachytherapy? How does tamoxifen affect radiation sensitivity?
cases.description: Decision about endometrial cancer in tamoxifen-treated breast cancer survivor

cases.input: I am treating a 45-year-old male with a 4 cm anal canal squamous cell carcinoma (Stage II, T2N0M0) who has a perianal abscess that was drained 2 weeks ago. He has persistent perianal inflammation. Should I delay chemoradiation until the abscess heals? What is the optimal timing? Should I consider brachytherapy boost? How does the perianal abscess affect treatment planning and outcomes?
cases.description: Decision about timing of chemoradiation after perianal abscess

cases.input: I have a 35-year-old female with a 3 cm vaginal cancer (squamous cell, Stage II) who is pregnant at 10 weeks gestation. She wants to continue the pregnancy. Her obstetric team supports continuing the pregnancy. Should I recommend neoadjuvant chemotherapy followed by brachytherapy during pregnancy? What chemotherapy agents are safe during pregnancy? What is the fetal dose from vaginal brachytherapy? When should delivery be planned?
cases.description: Complex decision for vaginal cancer during pregnancy

cases.input: I am evaluating a 70-year-old male with a 3 cm prostate cancer (Gleason 3+4, Stage T2a, PSA 7.8) who has a colostomy from prior rectal cancer treatment. He wants LDR brachytherapy. How does the colostomy affect perineal access for seed implantation? Should I use a transperineal approach? Are there any modifications needed for applicator placement? What are the infection risks with the colostomy nearby?
cases.description: Decision about prostate LDR brachytherapy with colostomy

cases.input: I have a 50-year-old female with a 4 cm vulvar melanoma (Stage II, Breslow 3.5 mm, ulcerated) who had a radical vulvectomy with bilateral inguinal lymph node dissection (1 of 8 nodes positive on right). She has a well-controlled seizure disorder on levetiracetam. She needs adjuvant radiation. Should I recommend adjuvant radiation to the vulvar bed and right inguinal region? Should I consider brachytherapy boost to the vulvar bed? What concurrent chemotherapy would be appropriate? How does levetiracetam interact with radiation therapy?
cases.description: Adjuvant therapy decision with seizure disorder comorbidity

cases.input: I am treating a 65-year-old male with a 3 cm prostate cancer (Gleason 4+3, Stage T3a, PSA 15) who has a heart transplant 5 years ago and is on chronic immunosuppression (tacrolimus, mycophenolate, prednisone). He wants definitive treatment. Given his immunosuppressed state, should I recommend brachytherapy, EBRT, or prostatectomy? How does immunosuppression affect radiation therapy? Should I modify the immunosuppressive regimen during treatment? What are the infection risks?
cases.description: Treatment decision for prostate cancer in heart transplant recipient
